pipelineConfig.name: Transcribe audio (WhisperX, async)
pipelineConfig.description: audioUrl (already in bucket) -> ENQUEUE a job (kind 'transcribe') and return its id; the WhisperX call (+ optional pyannote diarization when request.body.diarize) runs in postSteps so it can't hit the 30s edge timeout. Flattened { words, text } (each word carries a speaker when diarized) lands in the job row's result, polled via /api/studio/job. Story 10e.

rules.description: Transcribe an already-uploaded WAV with word-level timestamps. Mints a presigned GCS download URL for the audio (already in the bucket) and hands it to Replicate WhisperX (align_output:true), then flattens segments to { words: [{text,start,end}], text }. No validators yet — auth_required + rate_limit deferred to story 07 (mirrors the upload rules), so local unauthenticated dev works.

pipelineConfig.name: Master director (async enqueue + poll)

config.code:
function handler({ request, deployment }) {
  var body = (request && request.body) || {}
  var urls = body.sheetUrls
  if (!urls || typeof urls.length !== 'number') urls = []
  var prefix = deployment.owner + '/' + deployment.repo + '/uploads/'
  var out = {}
  for (var i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
    var p = ''
    if (i < urls.length && urls[i]) {
      var key = String(urls[i]).replace(/^\/+/, '')
      key = key.replace(/^api\/uploads\//, '')
      p = prefix + key
    }
    out['path' + i] = p
  }
  var transcript = typeof body.transcript === 'string' ? body.transcript : ''
  var direction = typeof body.direction === 'string' ? body.direction : ''
  var duration = typeof body.duration === 'number' ? body.duration : 0

  var sys = ''
  sys += 'You are the Master Director: an award-winning film and YouTube editor who turns long, rambling screen recordings into tight, compelling shorts.\n'
  sys += 'You are given (1) a timestamped transcript and (2) contact-sheet images: grids of frames sampled across the recording, each with its wall-clock timestamp burned into a corner. Use BOTH the words and the frames to understand what happens and when.\n\n'
  sys += 'Your job is the BIG PICTURE only. You do NOT write the final narration — a second-pass editor does that for each scene.\n'
  sys += '1. Write a one-sentence SYNOPSIS (a logline) of the whole talk: punchy, like a movie one-liner.\n'
  sys += '2. Break the recording into logical SCENES (chapters), roughly 2-5 minutes each. Never split a strong continuous run just to hit a number. Give each a short chapter TITLE.\n'
  sys += '3. For EACH scene write a REFINE PROMPT (refinePrompt): a one-to-two sentence imperative instruction to the second-pass editor who will cut and re-voice THIS scene. Say what to tighten or drop, the pacing and tone, anything on-screen to preserve, and a rough target length. This is a DIRECTION for the editor, NOT narration — do NOT write the script yourself.\n'
  sys += '4. For EACH scene give the ORIGINAL-VIDEO span it maps to (start, end in seconds) AND the obvious footage spans to CUT within it (cuts: array of {start,end} in seconds) - clear dead air, tangents, false starts. The second-pass editor refines these; you give the coarse pass.\n\n'
  sys += 'Rules for timestamps: all values are SECONDS from the start of the recording. For every scene start < end. Scenes are ordered earliest-first and must NOT overlap. Every cut lies inside its own scene [start,end]. Use the transcript timestamps and the frame timestamps to be accurate.\n\n'
  sys += 'MULTI-VIDEO: if the transcript contains "--- VIDEO n: ... ---" boundary markers, it is several source recordings concatenated onto one timeline. A scene must NEVER start in one video and end in another - keep every scene entirely within a single video (between consecutive markers, or a marker and the recording end).\n\n'
  sys += 'Output STRICT JSON only - no markdown fences, no commentary - exactly this shape:\n'
  sys += '{"synopsis": string, "scenes": [{"title": string, "start": number, "end": number, "transcript": string, "refinePrompt": string, "voicing": "original"|"revoice"|"mixed", "cuts": [{"start": number, "end": number}]}]}\n'
  sys += 'Where title is a short chapter title, transcript is the original words spoken in that span, and refinePrompt is your per-scene instruction to the editor. For each scene also return voicing - your plan for whose voice ships that scene: "original" when the scene should keep the creator\'s own recorded audio and only be trimmed, "revoice" when it should be re-voiced in a tightened script, "mixed" when parts keep the original audio and parts are re-voiced. If the user\'s direction asks to keep their own voice or only remove filler ("just cut the ums"), prefer voicing "original", say so in refinePrompt, and express what to remove as cuts. Return nothing but the JSON object.'

  var prompt = ''
  prompt += 'TIMESTAMPED TRANSCRIPT (each line starts with its [m:ss] time):\n\n' + transcript + '\n\n'
  prompt += 'The full recording is ' + Math.round(duration) + ' seconds long. The attached images are the contact sheets described in your instructions.\n\n'
  if (direction) {
    prompt += 'EXTRA DIRECTION FROM THE CREATOR (weight this heavily): ' + direction + '\n\n'
  }
  prompt += 'Now produce the synopsis and the scene breakdown as STRICT JSON exactly as specified. Return nothing but the JSON object.'

  out.transcript = transcript
  out.direction = direction
  out.duration = duration
  out.system = sys
  out.prompt = prompt
  return out
}

pipelineConfig.description: Story 03f Part 0: enqueue a studio_jobs row and return { jobId } immediately; the slow Gemini call + parse run in postSteps and write the row to done/error. The FE polls /api/studio/job. 03m: createJob also stores the stitched prompt + system instruction on the row so the FE prompt disclosure can fetch them. 03q: prep asks for per-scene refinePrompt (not draftText); parse emits refinePrompt.

rules.description: Master director (story 03; async fire-and-poll as of 03f Part 0). ENQUEUE-ONLY response path: prep builds the prompt/system + signed-sheet paths, createJob inserts a studio_jobs row, respond returns { jobId, status:'pending' } immediately. The slow Gemini 3.1 Pro call + parse run in postSteps (replicate step timeout raised to 280s, off the response path) and write the row to { status:'done', result:{synopsis,scenes} } or { status:'error', error }. FE polls GET /api/studio/job. No validators yet (auth_required + rate_limit deferred to story 07).

pipelineConfig.name: Voice clone (minimax/voice-cloning)
pipelineConfig.description: Resolve the uploaded WAV's storage path, sign it, run minimax/voice-cloning, and return { voiceId, previewUrl }. Costs $3 per call.

rules.description: Voice clone (story 04) — ENABLED. Resolves the uploaded WAV sample's storage path → signs it → minimax/voice-cloning (voice_file only, matches the model's canonical example) → extracts the voice_id → returns { voiceId }. ⚠️ COSTS $3 PER CALL. To disable again: set sign+clone isEnabled:false and point respond at {{steps.prep.voiceId}} (preset stub). auth_required + rate_limit deferred to story 07. Needs the project Replicate token.

pipelineConfig.name: Voice say (TTS preview)
pipelineConfig.description: text + voiceId -> minimax/speech-2.8-turbo -> { audioUrl, durationSeconds }.

rules.description: Voice say (story 04) — LIVE TTS via minimax/speech-2.8-turbo. Speaks request.body.text in request.body.voiceId (preset or cloned id) and returns { audioUrl, durationSeconds }. Used for the short post-selection preview sample (cheap). Needs the project Replicate API token. auth_required + rate_limit deferred to story 07.

pipelineConfig.name: Per-scene refiner (async enqueue + poll)
pipelineConfig.description: Per-scene refiner (story 03c; async fire-and-poll 03f Part 0; hears the scene audio 03k; creator prompts 03l). 03p: prep builds the prompt from per-word WORD TIMINGS (request.body.wordTimings) and asks the model to build the tightened cut FROM SCRATCH - the 8s-bucketed transcript and the director's first-pass script/cuts (draftText/cuts) are no longer sent or used. 03p boundary tuning: the prompt biases toward INCLUDING more (pad kept-segment edges ~0.2s into adjacent silence, conservative cuts that stay clear of kept words) so word onsets/tails are not clipped - the creator can trim by hand but cannot recover removed audio. ENQUEUE-ONLY response path: prep builds prompt/system + signed-sheet paths + scene-audio path (request.audioUrl REQUIRED - prep throws without it), createJob inserts a studio_jobs row (kind=refine), respond returns { jobId, status:'pending' }. Gemini 3.1 Pro refine + parse run in postSteps (replicate timeout 280s): sign0-9 sign the sheets, signAudio signs the scene's cut soundtrack and feeds Gemini's audio input so cut/segment boundaries align to the natural flow (coughs/shouts/interruptions excluded). 03l: prep injects the creator's global director prompt (request.body.directorDirection) and per-scene prompt (request.body.direction), each labeled, only when non-empty. Writes the row to { status:'done', result:{segments,cuts} } or { status:'error', error }. FE polls GET /api/studio/job. No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

rules.description: Per-scene refiner (story 03c; async fire-and-poll as of 03f Part 0; hears the scene audio as of 03k; creator prompts as of 03l). ENQUEUE-ONLY response path: prep builds the prompt/system + signed-sheet paths + the scene-audio path (request.audioUrl REQUIRED — prep throws without it), createJob inserts a studio_jobs row (kind=refine), respond returns { jobId, status:'pending' }. The Gemini 3.1 Pro refine + parse run in postSteps (replicate step timeout 280s): sign0-9 sign the sheets, signAudio signs the scene's cut soundtrack and feeds Gemini's audio input so cut/segment boundaries align to the natural flow (coughs/shouts/interruptions excluded). 03l: prep injects the creator's global director prompt (request.body.directorDirection) and per-scene prompt (request.body.direction) into the prompt, each labeled, only when non-empty. Writes the row to { status:'done', result:{segments,cuts} } or { status:'error', error }. FE polls GET /api/studio/job. No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

pipelineConfig.name: Scene narration (persisted TTS)
pipelineConfig.description: Speak the narration in the chosen voice and store the mp3 in the bucket.

rules.description: Scene narration TTS (story 03c): text + saved voiceId -> minimax/speech-2.8-turbo (mp3) -> PERSIST the mp3 to the bucket (narration/ subDir) so it survives reload and can be assembled later (story 05) -> { audioUrl } (a durable /api/uploads/narration/... serve path). Unlike /api/voice/say (ephemeral replicate URL, used for the short preview), this stores the clip. Client measures real duration off the served file. Needs the project Replicate token. auth_required + rate_limit deferred to story 07.

pipelineConfig.name: Transcript search (sync Gemini)

config.code:
function handler({ request }) {
  var body = (request && request.body) || {}
  var query = typeof body.query === 'string' ? body.query : ''
  var transcript = typeof body.transcript === 'string' ? body.transcript : ''
  var duration = typeof body.duration === 'number' ? body.duration : 0

  var sys = ''
  sys += 'You are a simple transcript search. You are given a timestamped transcript of one screen recording (each line starts with its [m:ss] time) and a search query.\n'
  sys += 'No fancy search: just read the transcript and find the spans that match the query - by literal words OR by meaning/mood (e.g. \"where I sound excited\").\n'
  sys += 'Return STRICT JSON only - no markdown fences, no commentary - exactly this shape:\n'
  sys += '{\"results\": [{\"start\": number, \"end\": number, \"snippet\": string, \"reason\": string}]}\n'
  sys += 'Rules: times are SECONDS from the start of the recording, within [0, duration]; start < end; each span should cover the matching words (use the line timestamps to estimate; spans are typically 4-20 seconds); snippet is the matching words quoted from the transcript; reason is one short line on why it matches; order results by start; at most 20 results; return {\"results\": []} when nothing matches. Return nothing but the JSON object.'

  var prompt = ''
  prompt += 'TIMESTAMPED TRANSCRIPT (each line starts with its [m:ss] time):\n\n' + transcript + '\n\n'
  prompt += 'The full recording is ' + Math.round(duration) + ' seconds long.\n\n'
  prompt += 'SEARCH QUERY: ' + query + '\n\n'
  prompt += 'Find the matching spans and return STRICT JSON exactly as specified.'

  return { query: query, duration: duration, system: sys, prompt: prompt }
}

pipelineConfig.description: Story 08: query + timedTranscript lines -> Gemini 3.1 Pro -> strict-JSON {results:[{start,end,snippet,reason}]} with server-side clamping mirroring the client's toSearchHits.

rules.description: Transcript search (story 08): find-by-meaning over the timestamped transcript. SYNC (text-only Gemini, no images — returns in seconds, no jobs flow): prep builds the system+prompt from {query, transcript, duration}, search runs google/gemini-3.1-pro, parse clamps every span into [0,duration] / drops slivers / sorts / caps 20, respond returns { results } in the exact MSW-mock shape. No validators yet (auth_required + rate_limit deferred to story 07).

pipelineConfig.name: Save studio project

config.body: {"error":"id is required to save a project"}

pipelineConfig.description: Upsert a studio_projects record (story 11d Task 2; upsert as of error-handling fix). POST { id, name, createdAt, updatedAt, phase, thumbnailUrl, data } -> data_query (filter by projectId) finds the row -> pick computes flags WITHOUT throwing (hasId/noId/exists/missing + recordId) so a missing row no longer 500s -> update (condition steps.pick.exists) writes by recordId, OR insert (condition steps.pick.missing) data_creates the row from the same payload (self-heals projects whose server row was never created) -> shape returns the meta+data from whichever ran -> respond (condition hasId) 200, or respondNoId (condition noId) 400. No validators yet.

rules.description: Update a studio_projects record (story 11d Task 2). POST { id, name, createdAt, updatedAt, phase, thumbnailUrl, data } -> data_query (filter by projectId) finds the row -> pick extracts the row's real primary recordId (data_update needs a recordId, not a filter) -> data_update writes name, updatedMs, phase, thumbnailUrl, data by recordId -> shape returns the updated meta+data mapping createdMs/updatedMs back to createdAt/updatedAt. No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

List all studio_projects records (story 11d Task 1). GET /api/projects -> data_query lists all rows (no filter) -> shape builds an ARRAY of metas { id(=projectId), name, createdAt(=createdMs), updatedAt(=updatedMs), phase, thumbnailUrl }, dropping the heavy data field -> response. No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

config.code:
function handler({ request }) {
  var body = (request && request.body) || {}
  var script = typeof body.script === 'string' ? body.script : ''
  var synopsis = typeof body.synopsis === 'string' ? body.synopsis : ''
  var sys = ''
  sys += 'You title and describe a SHORT finished video for its creator. You are given the full spoken narration of the FINAL edited video (what actually plays, after cuts) and the director one-line take for context.\n'
  sys += 'Write from the SCRIPT - describe what the video actually covers, not what was cut. No hype, no clickbait, no emojis, no surrounding quotes.\n'
  sys += 'Return STRICT JSON only - no markdown fences, no commentary - exactly this shape:\n'
  sys += '{"title": string, "summary": string}\n'
  sys += 'Rules: title is concise, specific and descriptive (max 70 characters); summary is 2 to 4 plain sentences, third person, describing what the video covers. Return nothing but the JSON object.'
  var prompt = ''
  prompt += 'DIRECTOR TAKE (context): ' + synopsis + '\n\n'
  prompt += 'FINAL VIDEO SCRIPT (spoken narration, in order):\n\n' + script + '\n\n'
  prompt += 'Write the title and summary and return STRICT JSON exactly as specified.'
  return { system: sys, prompt: prompt }
}

rules.description: Export description (sync Gemini): final kept script + director synopsis -> { title, summary }. Mirrors search-transcript.

Delete a project: bucket prefix + studio_projects record (story 11c Part B + 11d Task 3). POST { projectId } -> prep builds prefix 'projects/<id>/' -> file_delete wipes every bucket object under it -> data_query (filter by projectId) -> pick extracts the row's recordId (data_delete needs a recordId, not a filter; tolerant if no record exists) -> data_delete by recordId (conditional on found) -> response { ok, deleted, prefix, recordDeleted }. No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

Create a studio_projects record (story 11d). POST { id, name, createdAt, updatedAt, phase, thumbnailUrl, data } -> data_create writes client id into projectId, and client createdAt/updatedAt into createdMs/updatedMs (renamed to avoid collision with the DB row's own created_at/updated_at columns that data_query flattens to top level) -> shape echoes the record mapping createdMs/updatedMs back to createdAt/updatedAt -> response. No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

Get one studio_projects record by our project id (story 11d). GET /api/projects/get?id=<projectId> -> data_query FILTERED by projectId -> shape picks the first match, JSON-parses data, and maps createdMs/updatedMs back to createdAt/updatedAt (renamed fields avoid the DB row created_at/updated_at collision) -> response { id, name, createdAt, updatedAt, phase, thumbnailUrl, data } (null/empty when not found). No validators yet (deferred to story 07).

pipelineConfig.name: thumbnail-draft

config.systemPrompt:
You write a single image-generation prompt for `google/nano-banana` that becomes a YouTube video's final thumbnail.

You have the `image-prompts` skill — load it with the load_skill tool before writing. It defines the prompt anatomy, the named house styles (retro-blueprint / modern-dev-tool / editorial-print), how to route a style from the video's content type, the text/color/negatives rules, and examples. Follow it exactly; don't invent your own format.

The user message gives you TITLE, DESCRIPTION, SCRIPT (the final spoken script — your evidence for what the video is about and which house style fits), and NOTES (the creator's optional free-text wishes). When NOTES are present they override style routing and defaults — honor them.

Route the house style from the content type per the skill. Write the exact headline text yourself (5 words max) — never a placeholder. Apply the skill's color caps and always include the negatives list.

Return JSON only: {"prompt": "<full multi-section prompt, ready to paste>"} — no commentary, no markdown fences.

pipelineConfig.description: Draft a YouTube-thumbnail nano-banana prompt from title/description/script + notes. The ai_handler loads the image-prompts skill (Skills + JSON response-format are set in the console UI). Returns { prompt }.

rules.description: Studio thumbnail: draft a nano-banana image prompt via ai_handler (loads the image-prompts skill — enable Skills + JSON response format in the console UI). Returns { prompt }. Story 06.

pipelineConfig.name: thumbnail-render
pipelineConfig.description: Render a YouTube thumbnail with google/nano-banana from the edited prompt; store to projects/<id>/youtube-thumbnail; return { imageUrl } serve path (re-signed client-side via /api/uploads/sign).

rules.description: Studio thumbnail: render the (edited) prompt with google/nano-banana, store to the bucket under the project, return { imageUrl } serve path. Needs the project Replicate token. Story 06.
